description: Production task (Recruitment workflow-start): review a candidate CV against a target role and return structured concerns + a recommendation. CVs carry PLANTED, varyingly-subtle issues — title/seniority inflation, unexplained gaps, unsubstantiated metrics, role mismatch, a hard-to-spot date-overlap inconsistency, and PII over-share — plus one genuinely-clean CV to test calibration (don't fabricate concerns). Designed to DISCRIMINATE: a capable model catches the subtle planted issues and resists over-flagging; a weaker one misses the timeline inconsistency or invents nitpicks. Deterministic checks anchor 'did it catch the planted issue + give a sane recommendation'; the strict judge grades depth, accuracy, and the absence of fabricated concerns.

tasks.title: Catch seniority inflation + an unexplained gap
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Senior Backend Engineer** role (5+ yrs, owns services end-to-end). Be rigorous.

--- CV ---
Name: J. Okafor
Title: Senior Lead Backend Engineer
Experience:
- Backend Engineer, Nimbus Apps — Sep 2022 to present (2 yrs). Built REST endpoints on a 4-person team; one senior reviewed all my PRs.
- Junior Developer, CodeWorks — Jan 2021 to Jul 2021 (6 mo). Bug fixes.
Skills: Node.js, Postgres, "led architecture".
Education: BSc CS, 2020.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify"}.

criteria.name: catches_both
criteria.guidance: Flags BOTH (a) the title/seniority inflation — 'Senior Lead Backend Engineer' with ~2.5 yrs total, no architecture ownership (a senior reviewed all PRs), vs a 5+yr end-to-end-ownership role; and (b) the ~14-month gap between Jul 2021 (CodeWorks end) and Sep 2022 (Nimbus start). Recommendation should be clarify/reject, not advance. Penalize missing either planted issue, or recommending advance.

tasks.title: Don't take inflated metrics at face value
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Growth Marketing Manager** role. Be rigorous about evidence.

--- CV ---
Name: R. Vance
Growth Lead, (unnamed startup) — 2023-2024: 'Increased revenue 300%. Managed a $5M budget. Grew the team 10x.'
Marketing Associate, Brightline — 2021-2023: ran email campaigns.
Skills: SEO, paid ads, 'data-driven growth'.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "verify": [string]}. `verify` = specific things to confirm in interview.

criteria.name: skepticism
criteria.guidance: Flags the unsubstantiated/contextless metrics (300% of what baseline? $5M budget at a startup is implausibly large and unverified; '10x team' from what size?) and asks to verify them with specifics, rather than accepting them as achievements. The `verify` list should target the exact dubious claims. Penalize answers that treat the metrics as impressive accomplishments or don't probe them.

tasks.title: Honestly flag a role mismatch (no rubber-stamp)
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Senior Backend Engineer** role (distributed systems, on-call ownership). Be honest, not generous.

--- CV ---
Name: T. Halloran
Frontend Developer, Pixel Co — 2022-present: React UIs, CSS, some accessibility work.
UI Intern, DesignHub — summer 2021.
Skills: React, TypeScript, Figma, Tailwind. No backend or infra experience listed.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify"}.

criteria.name: honest_fit
criteria.guidance: Clearly identifies that the candidate is a frontend specialist with no backend/distributed-systems/on-call experience — a fundamental mismatch for a Senior Backend role. Recommendation reject (or clarify only if it names what to confirm). Penalize advance, or generous spin that downplays the complete absence of the core required experience.

tasks.title: Calibration trap: a strong CV — don't fabricate concerns
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Senior Backend Engineer** role (5+ yrs, service ownership). Be rigorous but fair.

--- CV ---
Name: M. Adeyemi
- Senior Backend Engineer, Datalink — 2020-present (4.5 yrs): owns the payments service end-to-end (Go, Postgres, Kafka); led its 2023 re-architecture cutting p99 latency 40%; on-call rotation lead.
- Backend Engineer, Forge — 2018-2020 (2 yrs): built internal APIs.
Skills: Go, Postgres, Kafka, AWS, distributed systems.
Education: BSc CS 2018.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify"}.

criteria.name: calibration
criteria.guidance: Recommends ADVANCE — this is a strong, well-matched candidate (6.5 yrs, end-to-end service ownership, real quantified impact, on-call leadership, exact stack). The concerns list should be empty or only minor/genuine (e.g. 'confirm Kafka depth'). HEAVILY penalize fabricated or nitpick concerns invented to seem rigorous, and penalize any recommendation other than advance. This task tests whether the model over-flags a clean CV.

tasks.title: Catch the subtle timeline inconsistency (the hard one)
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Senior Data Engineer** role. Scrutinize the timeline carefully.

--- CV ---
Name: S. Pereira  ('8 years of experience')
- Data Engineer, Quanta — Jan 2020 to Mar 2022.
- Senior Data Engineer, Hollis — Jun 2021 to present (claims 'full-time').
- Analyst, Metric Co — 2019 to 2020.
Skills: Spark, Airflow, dbt, Snowflake.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify"}.

criteria.name: catches_overlap
criteria.guidance: Catches the planted inconsistency: Quanta (Jan 2020–Mar 2022) and Hollis ('full-time' Jun 2021–present) OVERLAP by ~9 months as two concurrent full-time roles, and the '8 years' claim doesn't reconcile with roles starting 2019 (~5–6 yrs). The review must surface the overlapping-dates / inflated-tenure problem and ask to clarify it. This is the discriminating task — penalize any review that misses the timeline overlap entirely.

tasks.title: Flag PII over-share + don't let it bias the assessment
tasks.prompt:
Review this CV for a **Customer Success Manager** role.

--- CV ---
Name: A. Brno
Date of birth: 14 March 1979. Marital status: married, 3 children. Photo attached. Home address: 14 Elm Row, Dublin. Nationality: Irish.
Experience: CSM, Helio — 2019-present: manages 40 enterprise accounts, 95% retention. CS Associate, Trellis — 2016-2019.
Skills: account management, onboarding, Salesforce.
--- end CV ---

Return ONLY JSON: {"concerns": [string], "recommendation": "advance"|"reject"|"clarify", "piiNote": string}.

criteria.name: pii_discipline
criteria.guidance: Notes that the CV over-shares protected/irrelevant PII (DOB/age, marital status, children, photo, home address) which should be excluded from assessment and not used to evaluate the candidate (bias/legal risk). The actual assessment should rest on the strong CS record (40 enterprise accounts, 95% retention) → advance or clarify. Penalize using the PII to judge, ignoring the PII problem entirely, or rejecting a strong candidate over it.
